deferred_scope: src/slm_training/harnesses/quality/ curriculum/retrieval modules (curriculum stage weights, retrieval-bank scoring vectors) -- not read in this pass.

mixture_manifest-driven quota scalars (mixture_exposure_target_profile, mixture_total_decision_budget, mixture_per_root_cap, mixture_per_template_cap, mixture_max_importance_weight) -- these are scalars/paths on ModelBuildConfig, not vectors; the *contents* of an external mixture_manifest JSON file are not schema-validated by this change.

grammar_diffusion.py / tree_edit_diffusion.py own config surfaces (topology_max_*, block_size, production/slot/confidence_loss_weight, scope_* flags) -- separate model families from TwoTower; out of scope per this issue's TwoTower-scoped non-goals.

~30 individual semantic_plan_*/schema_*/binder_*/root_reference_*/component_* per-role float decode-weight SCALARS on TwoTowerConfig -- each is in-scope conceptually (a 'per-role weight') but has no vector shape or cross-field pairing to validate beyond finite/non-negative; a full per-field capability audit of all ~30 was not completed in this pass.

action_alias_manifest / targeted_margin_manifest / mixture_manifest Path fields -- only presence/type is implied by TwoTowerConfig's own type hints; external file *schema* validation is not part of this change.
src/slm_training/harnesses/model_build/checkpoint_path_manifest.py and decode_path.py -- read during this audit; contain no vector/schedule-shaped config fields, so nothing to add there.

fields.capability_rule: non-empty vector requires denoiser_arch == 'shared_recursive' (the only arch implementing recursive_outputs).
fields.semantics: Per-recursion-depth cross-entropy weights for SLM-138 deep supervision over SharedRecursiveDenoiserTower.recursive_outputs; empty tuple = feature off.
fields.validation_rule: non-empty vectors: every weight finite & >=0 (positive_sum_vector rejects all-zero), length == recursive_steps exactly (exact_length_vector, rejects silent min()-truncation).

fields.capability_rule: none (always usable whenever LTR decode runs; no separate enable flag gates the vector itself).
fields.semantics: Progressive left-to-right decode canvas stage lengths (short canvases first). None means 'use the built-in default (64,128,192,256)'.
fields.validation_rule: None always valid; else non-empty, every stage an int > 1 (stages <=1 are silently dropped by _ltr_canvas_stages's filter), strictly increasing (no duplicates/unsorted).

fields.capability_rule: none gated -- shape validated unconditionally; only ever read when mask_pattern=='diffusion' (benign default, not gated -- see found defect #2 discussion for the sibling weight field).
fields.semantics: Online corruption-policy mixture consumed by DiffusionConfig/corrupt_batch when mask_pattern=='diffusion'.
fields.validation_rule: non-empty, unique, every value in the known POLICIES set (slm_training.data.diffusion.adapter.POLICIES).

fields.semantics: Length-bucket boundaries sizing the diffusion length-prediction head (self.length_head), built only when mask_pattern=='diffusion'.
fields.validation_rule: non-empty, positive ints, strictly increasing (stricter than DiffusionConfig's own 'sorted, dup ok' check).

fields.capability_rule: DELIBERATELY NOT GATED on mask_pattern=='diffusion' -- see found defect #2: the shipped default (0.1) is already inert under the shipped default mask_pattern ('random'); a capability rule here would reject TwoTowerConfig()/ModelBuildConfig() themselves, which the issue's non-goals forbid working around by changing the default.
fields.semantics: Coefficient for the diffusion length-prediction auxiliary loss term.
fields.status: shape-only; capability check deliberately omitted (documented false-positive risk)

fields.semantics: Paired scalars bounding the per-row uniform mask-rate sample used by both random masking (_mask_targets) and DiffusionConfig.

fields.capability_rule: none written -- see found defect #3: the field has zero readers anywhere in twotower.py; it is dead configuration, so there is no downstream consumer to gate against.
fields.semantics: Per-family confusion-margin weight table, intended to pair with legal_margin_mode's targeted contrastive margin.
fields.validation_rule: unique family keys, every weight finite & >= 0.

fields.capability_rule: non-empty vector requires slot_component_loss_weight > 0 or slot_component_decode_weight > 0 (slot-component head enabled).
fields.semantics: Per-class cross-entropy weight for the slot-component head; computed internally by TwoTowerModel.from_records (class-balanced weighting), not user-authored.
fields.status: covered; length-vs-class-count check deferred (needs the built tokenizer's component inventory, unavailable at config-construction time, and the field is not user-reachable so the risk is low -- see design note).
fields.validation_rule: non-negative finite entries, positive sum (all-zero rejected).

fields.capability_rule: none written -- weight>0-with-empty-table is a legitimate small-corpus outcome the runtime already treats as an intentional no-op (see design note; a capability rule here would false-positive against normal training).
fields.semantics: token -> per-class log-prior score table, mined from the training corpus, additively biasing slot-component logits when slot_component_lexeme_prior_weight > 0.
fields.validation_rule: unique keys, every key's score vector the same length as every other key's (paired_equal_length_sequences), all values finite.

fields.semantics: (role-pair key) -> per-class log-prior score table, mined from the training corpus, additively biasing slot-component logits when slot_component_span_prior_weight > 0.

scope_statement: Full audit of TwoTowerConfig's own numeric vector/schedule config surface (models/twotower.py) plus the ModelBuildConfig fields that mirror it (harnesses/model_build/config.py) and the factory/override/checkpoint call sites that construct or mutate a TwoTowerConfig. Explicitly does NOT claim coverage of grammar_diffusion.py, tree_edit_diffusion.py, or the quality/curriculum harness's own vector-shaped fields -- see deferred_scope.
